rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the intended target component or implementation while preserving AADL architecture
Place component type annexes in valid component type scopes and implementation annexes in implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections
Use visible identifiers from the current visible identifiers list when available
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Ensure AGREE annex blocks are placed in valid scopes according to AADL rules
Use visible identifiers from the current visible identifiers list when available
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, `const`, and `assign` syntax for AGREE contracts
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees inside annex agree only when the case supplies concrete bounds, named constants, or a visible relation
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for them
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
